case_id: Northern California region (WGS84 coordinates around latitude 37.5-37.8°N, longitude -122.5 to -121.9°W)

pi_items.source: setup_qgis.sh (lines 61-86) generates this file with two rectangular polygons (Area A and Area B) in WGS84 coordinates
setup_qgis.sh (lines 89-101) generates this file with three points (Point A, B, C) in WGS84 coordinates
Task description and export_result.sh (line 21) specify this output path
Task description states '1 kilometer (1000 meters)' exclusion zone. Computation confirms π × (1000m)² ÷ 1,000,000 = 3.14 sq km per buffer.

Web search (epsg.io) confirms EPSG:32610 is 'WGS 84 / UTM zone 10N' covering 126°W to 120°W. Task coordinates at -122°W are within this zone. Task description explicitly requires reprojection to EPSG:32610.

privileged_info_summary: Synthetic data generated by setup_qgis.sh: two WGS84 polygon features (Area A and B, each approximately 881 sq km when reprojected to UTM) and three point features (Points A, B, C). Task requires 1000-meter buffer zones around points, difference operation to exclude buffers from polygons, and output in GeoJSON format. EPSG:32610 (UTM Zone 10N) is the correct target CRS for the San Francisco Bay Area coordinates used. Expected output path is /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/developable_areas.geojson.
